46%
on The Trade Desk
Executive summary
10 deals across 4 sellers received 485.7M bid requests in the last 7 days but produced no bids — almost entirely a buyer-side activation issue. The Trade Desk accounts for 46% of unanswered requests; AdX carries 53% on the supply side. Top advertiser by silent volume: RON (161.5M requests across 2 deals). Ivy Lee has the longest list (3 deals). Sellers below are ordered by deal count, with each book broken out by SSP → DSP → Agency → Advertiser so the dark spots are easy to spot.

Need the full data?
All 10 deals are inline below, ordered by seller. For sortable/filterable access, use one of these:
⬇ Download CSV
Open dashboard →
Methodology. Auction deals (PA / PD) receiving bid requests but no bids over a 7-day window. Deals must have existed for at least 90 days (we don't flag deals buyers haven't had time to wire up). GAM PD threshold: ≥7 days in data and ≥100K bid requests (PDs have first-look optionality so low-volume zero-bid deals are noise). GAM PA and Magnite have no volume threshold. SSP attribution comes from the data source (Magnite / GAM / Pubmatic), not the deal name — Pubmatic deals with legacy naming still appear under Pubmatic. 1 deal have a deal-name SSP slot that looks wrong (DSP or “SSP” placeholder in slot 3) — they're still grouped under their real SSP above, but the names should be flagged to AdOps for renaming.
